title: Stelis oblonga
intro: Stelis oblonga is a species of orchid, commonly known as the "bitter orchid" due to its bitter taste and astringent properties. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Stelis oblonga include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis oblonga is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Stelis oblonga is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Stelis, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. The genus Stelis is further divided into several subgenera, with Stelis oblonga being a part of the subgenus Pleurothallis. The classification of Stelis oblonga is based on its physical characteristics, such as its flower shape and size. This classification helps scientists to understand the evolutionary relationships between different species within the Stelis genus.
